title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and alignment methods rather than major policy actions. The strongest updates focus on how to assess frontier systems more credibly, reduce safety-performance tradeoffs, harden agent ecosystems against indirect attacks, and address bias in model-based judging.

items.title: OpenAI outlines a playbook for trustworthy third-party evaluations
items.why_it_matters: It could shape external audits of frontier model capabilities, safeguards, and evaluation validity.

items.title: OpenAI publishes its views on AI policy and political advocacy
items.why_it_matters: It signals how a major lab frames regulation, transparency, and its public policy posture.

items.title: NIST expands its AI consortium and seeks new members
items.why_it_matters: Broader work on measurement science and evaluation can influence standards and assurance practices.

items.title: SafeSteer proposes localized on-policy distillation for safety alignment
items.why_it_matters: It targets the alignment tax by isolating safety updates to reduce general capability loss.

items.title: Researchers mitigate perceptual bias in multimodal LLM-as-a-judge systems
items.why_it_matters: More reliable automated judging improves evaluation quality where visual evidence conflicts with text.

items.title: SkillHarm studies automated skill-based attacks across agent lifecycles
items.why_it_matters: It highlights third-party skills as a persistent attack surface in agent deployment pipelines.

items.title: Tracking the Behavioral Trajectories of Adapting Agents
items.why_it_matters: Behavior-tracking methods could improve oversight of agents that self-modify through files and memory.

items.title: Pluralistic Leaderboards argues single rankings can hide preference differences
items.why_it_matters: It challenges one-score leaderboards that may misrepresent heterogeneous user values in model evaluation.

items.title: IntraShuffler proposes privacy-preserving heterogeneous DP federated learning
items.why_it_matters: It addresses privacy leakage risks when federated systems mix clients with different privacy budgets.

items.title: FigSIM introduces a dataset on suicide severity and figurative language in memes
items.why_it_matters: Better datasets can improve moderation of nuanced self-harm content that current systems miss.

items.title: Drifting Preference Optimization adapts alignment for one-step generative models
items.why_it_matters: It extends preference-based alignment to fast image generators that are harder to fine-tune safely.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
